description:
You are the DevOps engineer in charge of a very small “artifact-manager” service whose only job is to curate on-disk binary repositories under /home/user/artifact-manager.  
  A minimal configuration file already exists at  

      /home/user/artifact-manager/artifact-manager.conf  

  with this exact content (do **not** alter existing lines, only append new ones):  

      # Artifact Manager Configuration
      [global]
      storage_root = /home/user/artifact-manager/repos

  Goal: register a new, compressed-binary repository and produce a short verification log.

  Required end-state of the system
  1. Directory structure  
     • A new directory **/home/user/artifact-manager/repos/compressed** must exist.  
  2. Configuration update  
     • Append the following four lines (including the surrounding square-bracket section header) to **/home/user/artifact-manager/artifact-manager.conf**.  
       The order, spelling and white-space must be **identical** to what is shown here:  

           [repository "compressed"]
           type = binary
           path = /home/user/artifact-manager/repos/compressed
           retention_days = 90

     • Do not insert any blank lines before, after or between the new lines.  
  3. Verification log  
     • Create a plain-text file **/home/user/verification.log** that contains **exactly** the result of running  

           grep -A3 '\[repository "compressed"\]' /home/user/artifact-manager/artifact-manager.conf

       against the final version of the configuration file.  
     • The log must therefore contain **exactly** the four lines listed in point 2 in the same order and with a trailing newline after the last line.

  Constraints
  • Accomplish everything with at most **three shell commands** (directory creation, configuration append, and log creation).
  • You do **not** have sudo privileges.  
  • Perform all actions from the Linux terminal; do not open external editors.  
  • The automated test will check:  
      – existence of /home/user/artifact-manager/repos/compressed (it must be a directory)  
      – presence and exact content of the four appended lines in /home/user/artifact-manager/artifact-manager.conf  
      – exact four-line content of /home/user/verification.log (no extra spaces or blank lines)  
  • If you need to verify your work manually, you may output terminal commands, but the final artifact and log must match the specifications above.

  Deliverable: once finished, the three checks listed above should all succeed.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:
    • /home/user/artifact-manager/  (directory, already present)
    • /home/user/artifact-manager/repos/  (directory, already present)
    • /home/user/artifact-manager/artifact-manager.conf  (file, content exactly):
         # Artifact Manager Configuration
         [global]
         storage_root = /home/user/artifact-manager/repos

  Expected final state for automated grading:
    1. /home/user/artifact-manager/repos/compressed  exists and is a directory.
    2. /home/user/artifact-manager/artifact-manager.conf  (entire file content):
         # Artifact Manager Configuration
         [global]
         storage_root = /home/user/artifact-manager/repos
         [repository "compressed"]
         type = binary
         path = /home/user/artifact-manager/repos/compressed
         retention_days = 90
       (The file therefore has exactly 7 lines, the last being "retention_days = 90\n")
    3. /home/user/verification.log (file content, exactly):
         [repository "compressed"]
         type = binary
         path = /home/user/artifact-manager/repos/compressed
         retention_days = 90
       (Ends with newline after the last line.)
